creator: jfc@mit.edu
text: My first impression is clang is within its rights to break this code.  The JS implementation tries to store metadata bits in the low bits of a pointer type.  The compiler is allowed to assume that the low bits of a pointer value are zero. (Assuming the pointed-to type has alignment rules, which is normally the case.)  LLVM itself uses a wrapper class instead of an illegal pointer value.  The wrapper class stores the pointer+tag combination as an integer and provides a getter method to return an untagged pointer.
